request.question: What is the second project of the vocalist of the band The Distillers?

title: FOCSA Building
Dol Theeta
Accidents Never Happen
The Distillers
Hristo Botev Stadium (Vratsa)
The ROADEX Project
VentureCrowd
Brody Dalle
Virginia HOT lanes
Catherine Hickson
sentences: In an era when concrete construction of buildings more than 18 storeys tall was considered unaffordable, the FOCSA was only the second project of its kind in the world.
The project was managed by engineer, Luis Sáenz Duplace; the architect was Ernesto Gómez Sampera (1921–2004), who later moved to Puerto Rico where he worked until his death in 2004.
Dol Theeta is a progressive metal band formed in 2008 by Greek music composer Thanasis Lightbridge.
The band represents the second project of Lightbridge's "Electronica Art Metal" vision, the first project being Dol Ammad a more powerful and epic progressive project.
Dol Theeta has a more atmospheric and psychedelic character and combines diverse musical styles such as heavy metal, electronica and opera.
Accidents Never Happen (ANH) was a rock band from Oslo, Norway, formed in 2005.

After releasing their debut album "Oslo Beat" in 2006, vocalist and main songwriter Magne Mostue, guitarist Martin Natvig, drummer Christian Løvhaug and guitarist Jesper Jørgensen moved to Liverpool, UK.
The band's music was influenced significantly by their two-year stay in the UK.

Their second project, "Accidents Never Happen", was recorded in The Motor Museum and St Brides church; (The band's rehearsal space) with Simon Cullwick (who allegedly turned down an offer to work on Razorlight's "Slipway Fires").
"Accidents Never Happen" was a much darker album than "Oslo Beat".
The Distillers were an Australian-American punk rock band, formed in Los Angeles, California in 1998 by vocalist and guitarist Brody Dalle.
Dalle co-wrote, played guitar and provided vocals for nearly every track on the band's three albums.
After the breakup of the band in 2006, Dalle and Distillers guitarist Tony Bevilacqua went on to form Spinnerette.
Hristo Botev Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium in Vratsa, Bulgaria.
It is currently used mostly for football matches.
It has been the home of Botev Vratsa for 60 years.
The stadium was built in 1948 and has a seating capacity of 8,000 spectators.
After the approval of a project worth € 150 000, in 2008 the stadium was renovated and had 3,000 seats, spread in the 3 central sectors.
In June 2009, the Botev Vratsa management approved a second project, worth € 120 000 for reconstruction of sector "V", opposite the central building of the stadium.
The second renovation was completed in 2009.

The ROADEX Project is a technical co-operation between roads organisations across northern Europe part funded by the EU Northern Periphery Programme that aims to share roads related information and research between the partners.

The Project was started in 1998 as a 3 year pilot co-operation between the roads districts of Finnish Lapland, Troms County of Norway, the Northern Region of Sweden and the Highland Council of Scotland and was subsequently followed and extended by a second project, ROADEX II, from 2002 to 2005, a third project, ROADEX III, from 2006 to 2007, and a final project, “ROADEX Network Implementing Accessibility”, from 2009 to 2012.
VentureCrowd is an Australian multi asset class crowdfunding platform, headquartered in Sydney, including equity crowdfunding, property crowdfunding and debt-based crowdfunding.
VentureCrowd completed the largest Australian equity crowdfunding raise, $4.2 million, for taxi-booking and payment software company Ingogo in May 2015.
This deal is ranked 8th on the List of highest funded equity crowdfunding projects.
This deal is ranked 8th on the List of highest funded equity crowdfunding projects.
In June 2016, VentureCrowd raised more than $900,000 for a Western Sydney residential project - a 35-lot development in Riverstone East, in partnership with the property developer ClearState.
In August 2016, a second project raised $1,700,000 for a 44-lot development project in Austral.
Brody Dalle (born Bree Joanna Alice Robinson; 1 January 1979) is an Australian-born singer-songwriter and guitarist.
Dalle began playing music in her adolescence, and moved to Los Angeles, California at age eighteen, where she found the punk rock band The Distillers.
The group released three albums before disbanding in 2006, and Dalle began another project, Spinnerette, releasing an eponymous album in 2009.
In 2014, she released "Diploid Love", her first album under her solo name.
Virginia HO/T lanes refers to five separate projects in the U.S. state of Virginia.
The first project, completed in November 2012, has added high-occupancy/toll (HO/T) lanes to the Capital Beltway (I-495) in Fairfax County.

The second project, opened to the public in December 2014, involved converting and extending the existing reversible high-occupancy vehicle (HOV) lanes on I-95 and I-395 to HO/T lanes from Stafford to near Alexandria.
The third project, currently under construction (as of December 2016), will convert the existing high-occupancy vehicle (HOV) lanes inside the beltway on Interstate 66 to HO/T lanes.
The fourth project will convert the existing high-occupancy vehicle (HOV) lane outside the beltway on Interstate 66 and add a second lane to HO/T lanes.
The fifth project will convert the existing reversible high-occupancy vehicle (HOV) lanes on I-395 to HO/T lanes from near Alexandria to Washington, DC.
The sixth project will convert the existing reversible HOV Lanes to HOT Lanes on Interstate 64 from Interstate 564 in Norfolk to Interstate 264 in Virginia Beach.
Catherine Hickson (born in 1955) is a Canadian volcanologist formerly part of the Geological Survey of Canada.
Hickson has mapped and studied young volcanoes in Canada assessing their past and future eruptions.
In 2003, Hickson was one of the three scientists to report on a hypothetical eruption at the Canadian volcano Mount Cayley.
Hickson works in South America where some of the largest and highest volcanoes are, including those in Argentina, Bolivia, Chile and Peru.
She was the creator and leader of the Multinational Andean Project.
The project ran from 1996 - 2002, with Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, and Peru, being co-funded by the Canadian International Development Agency and the participating countries.
In 2002 a second project was started that focused on volcanoes, earthquake and landslides and was expanded to include Ecuador, Colombia, and Venezuela.
It ended in 2006.

The work garnered Hickson the prestigious C.J. Westerman Memorial Award (2010) presented by the Association of Professional Engineers and Geoscientists of BC, and the Head of the Public Service Award (1999) among other awards.

In addition to her scientific contributions, she has made contributions to many publications for young people and the public, as well as being a sought after lecturer, field trip leader and supporter of science for the public.
She was an eyewitness to the 1980 eruption of Mount St. Helens and wrote a book in 2005 describing her experience and explaining the mechanism of the eruption for the public.
She now works in the private sector.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- When the context contains a concise bridge claim about a named entity and at least one passage provides an explicit, identity-aligned statement linking that same named entity (or a clearly indicated alias) to the asserted relation, append exactly one brief corroborating sentence that paraphrases that passage's linking fact and references the corroborating passage. Perform an explicit identity-alignment check (alias or explicit linking); do not treat mere name co-occurrence or a passage that merely mentions the name as sufficient. Do not append this corroboration if the `context` already contains the identical corroborating fact verbatim. Keep this addition short and keep the overall summary compact.
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.
- When the summaries contain both a short single-token alias and, in the later integrated summary (summary_2), a contiguous multi-token full-name literal that explicitly identifies or is explicitly linked to the same person (for example via an identity link or biographical framing in summary_2), prefer and emit that multi-token full-name literal exactly as it appears in summary_2. Do not prefer the longer form unless summary_2 provides an explicit identity link tying the alias to the full-name literal; if no such explicit link is present, follow the base identity-link rules and do not force selection of the longer form.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: What is the second project of the vocalist of the band The Distillers?
hop1_docs: The Distillers | The Distillers were an Australian-American punk rock band, formed in Los Angeles, California in 1998 by vocalist and guitarist Brody Dalle.  Dalle co-wrote, played guitar and provided vocals for nearly every track on the band's three albums.  After the breakup of the band in 2006, Dalle and Distillers guitarist Tony Bevilacqua went on to form Spinnerette.

Spinnerette | Spinnerette is an alternative rock band formed in 2007.  The band consists of Brody Dalle (the Distillers), Tony Bevilacqua (the Distillers), Jack Irons (What Is This? , Red Hot Chili Peppers, Walk the Moon, Eleven, Pearl Jam) and Alain Johannes (What Is This? , Walk the Moon, Eleven, Queens of the Stone Age, Them Crooked Vultures).  The band has been inactive since 2010 and its future is uncertain.

What Do I Do Now? | "What Do I Do Now?"  is a song by English Britpop band Sleeper, written by the band's vocalist and guitarist Louise Wener.  It was the first single to be released from their second album "The It Girl" in 1995 (see 1995 in British music).

Sound of Guns | Sound of Guns are an alternative rock band from Liverpool, England formed in 2008.  Discovered by "BBC Introducing" the band went on to sign with independent record label Distiller Records through which they released their debut album "What Came From Fire" on 28 June 2010 and second album "Angels and Enemies" on 5 March 2012.

Come What(ever) May | Come What(ever) May is the second studio album by American heavy metal band Stone Sour.  It was recorded and produced by the band and Nick Raskulinecz at Studio 606 in Los Angeles, California, and was released on August 1, 2006, by Roadrunner Records.  Writing for the album began as early as 2003 when vocalist Corey Taylor and guitarist James Root were writing material for their other band, Slipknot.  In January 2006, Stone Sour began recording their second album, during which time drummer Joel Ekman left the band because of family constraints.  He was eventually replaced by ex-Soulfly drummer Roy Mayorga who played on all but two tracks on the album.

Tony Martin (British singer) | Anthony Philip Harford (born 19 April 1957), better known by his stage name Tony Martin, is a heavy metal vocalist, best known for his time fronting Black Sabbath, initially from 1987 to 1991 and again from 1993 to 1997.  Martin was the band's second longest serving vocalist after Ozzy Osbourne.  He has since been involved in many other projects (such as M3, Misha Calvin, The Cage, Giuntini Project, and Phenomena).

That's What Friends Are For (Slade song) | That's What Friends Are For is a song by the British rock band Slade, released in 1987 as the second single from their fourteenth studio album "You Boyz Make Big Noize".  The song was written by lead vocalist Noddy Holder and bassist Jim Lea, and produced by Roy Thomas Baker.  It reached No. 95 in the UK, remaining in the charts for the one week.
hop1_titles: That's What Friends Are For (Slade song)
response.summary_1: Evidence: Brody Dalle is identified as "vocalist and guitarist Brody Dalle" of The Distillers, and "Dalle ... went on to form Spinnerette" with Tony Bevilacqua. Missing/bridge: passages do not provide a full chronology or list of Brody Dalle's projects to confirm which one is her "second project." Next hop: retrieve Brody Dalle's biography or discography (career timeline or list of bands/projects and dates) to determine the ordering of her projects and confirm which is her second.
response.hop2_query: Brody Dalle biography bands projects chronology Distillers Spinnerette list
hop2_docs: The Distillers | The Distillers were an Australian-American punk rock band, formed in Los Angeles, California in 1998 by vocalist and guitarist Brody Dalle.  Dalle co-wrote, played guitar and provided vocals for nearly every track on the band's three albums.  After the breakup of the band in 2006, Dalle and Distillers guitarist Tony Bevilacqua went on to form Spinnerette.

Brody Dalle | Brody Dalle (born Bree Joanna Alice Robinson; 1 January 1979) is an Australian-born singer-songwriter and guitarist.  Dalle began playing music in her adolescence, and moved to Los Angeles, California at age eighteen, where she found the punk rock band The Distillers.  The group released three albums before disbanding in 2006, and Dalle began another project, Spinnerette, releasing an eponymous album in 2009.  In 2014, she released "Diploid Love", her first album under her solo name.

Spinnerette | Spinnerette is an alternative rock band formed in 2007.  The band consists of Brody Dalle (the Distillers), Tony Bevilacqua (the Distillers), Jack Irons (What Is This? , Red Hot Chili Peppers, Walk the Moon, Eleven, Pearl Jam) and Alain Johannes (What Is This? , Walk the Moon, Eleven, Queens of the Stone Age, Them Crooked Vultures).  The band has been inactive since 2010 and its future is uncertain.

Diploid Love | Diploid Love is the debut solo album by Brody Dalle, former frontwoman of The Distillers and Spinnerette.  The digital download was released on April 28, 2014 in the United States and April 29 in Canada, followed by the CD and vinyl record set on May 19.  The lead single, "Meet the Foetus/Oh the Joy", was streamed for free on Soundcloud on February 9 before being released to iTunes on February 17.

Girls Talk (Garbage song) | "Girls Talk" is a stand-alone single released by alternative rock band Garbage for Record Store Day 2014.  Developed from a long gestating song idea, written by the band for inclusion on their 2007 greatest hits set "Absolute Garbage", "Girls Talk" features Brody Dalle (ex-The Distillers/Spinnerette).  Butch Vig described the original sessions for the song (then known as "Girls Talk Shit") as "pretty cool sounding, lots of fast pizzicato guitars and cellos".  The newly recorded take of "Girls Talk" features Eric Avery on bass guitar and was engineered and mixed by Billy Bush.

David Hidalgo Jr. | David Hidalgo Jr. (born August 30, 1984) is an American drummer currently playing in Social Distortion.  Hidalgo replaced former drummer Scott Reeder, who had been busy with his main project Fu Manchu.  Prior to joining Social Distortion, he played drums in The Drips and Suicidal Tendencies.  He also plays drums for The Bronx/Mariachi El Bronx and the hardcore punk band Bullet Treatment with a variety of lineups that also included members of Rise Against, The Bronx, Cancer Bats, Anti-Flag and others.  David also toured with Brody Dalle in her Spinnerette and self-titled projects.  He has recorded and toured with Chuck Ragan and Dave Hause.  Most recently, Dave recorded on Greg Graffin's new solo record.  He is the son of David Hidalgo, guitarist and singer of Los Lobos.

Tony Bevilacqua | Anthony Peter Bevilacqua (born July 23, 1976) is an American musician.  He is currently guitarist with Spinnerette and was previously a member of The Distillers, appearing on the band's third album and major label debut Coral Fang, which entered the US Billboard Album Top 200 Chart at #96 in October, 2003.  He also used the stage name "Tony Bradley" while with The Distillers.
response.summary_2: Brody Dalle's second project was Spinnerette. Passage [2] corroborates this, stating Dalle "began another project, Spinnerette," which released an eponymous album in 2009.
